name: Vanuatu (Republic of Vanuatu / Ripablik Blong Vanuatu)
summary: Vanuatu is a Pacific Island mixed-legal-system republic combining English common-law substantive heritage (via Anglo-French Condominium 1906-1980 hybrid inheritance) with French civil-law substantive influence and constitutionally-recognised kastom (customary-law) personal-status jurisdiction operating through Island Courts. The hybrid Anglo-French legal-system inheritance is structurally distinctive globally — Vanuatu is the only state in the corpus with explicit Anglo-French dual-colonial-inheritance heritage. Family-law framework operates under the Control of Marriage Act, the Matrimonial Causes Act, the Maintenance of Children Act, the Family Protection Act 2008, and customary-law applied via Island Courts. Parental responsibility and child custody are governed by case-law applying the welfare-of-the-child principle. The Court of Appeal of Vanuatu is the apex court for civil and criminal matters. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Supreme Court (Family Division), Magistrates' Courts, and Island Courts for kastom matters. Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework. Vanuatu is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the welfare-of-the-child principle. Vanuatu is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Family Protection Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute on family protection orders affecting family-law proceedings.

statutory_framework.title: Island Courts Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute establishing Island Courts for kastom (customary-law) jurisdiction.

apex_courts.name: Court of Appeal of Vanuatu
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health, Vanuatu
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Vanuatu family-court decisions are anonymised per Court of Appeal practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Anglo-French Condominium established
key_developments.description: Anglo-French Condominium established 1906 over the New Hebrides — substantively distinctive globally only-Anglo-French-Condominium-in-history framework. Substantive dual-administration-dual-legal-system framework persisting through 1980 independence. Foundational dual-colonial-inheritance framework affecting subsequent legal-system structure.

key_developments.title: Vanuatu independence + Walter Lini founding-prime-minister + Republic framework
key_developments.description: Vanuatu achieved independence 30 July 1980 from Anglo-French Condominium — Walter Lini (Vanua'aku Pati) first prime minister 1980-1991. Retained both English common-law and French civil-law substantive influences. Substantively distinctive globally Anglo-French-Condominium-independence framework. Foundational substantive-constitutional anchor.

key_developments.title: Island Courts Act + kastom-customary-law framework
key_developments.description: Federal statute Island Courts Act 1983 establishing Island Courts for kastom (customary-law) jurisdiction. Substantively significant Pacific Island kastom-recognition framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Vanuatu ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Vanuatu ratified the UNCRC on 7 July 1993 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2008 Family Protection Act substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Family Protection Act
key_developments.description: Federal statute on family protection orders enacted Family Protection Act 2008 — substantively distinctive Pacific Island domestic-violence-protection framework. Substantive child-protection-framework consolidation.

key_developments.title: Cyclone Pam + climate-disaster-vulnerability framework
key_developments.description: Cyclone Pam 13 March 2015 substantively devastated Vanuatu — substantively distinctive Pacific Category-5 climate-disaster framework. Substantive demographic and family-law-implementation disruption through post-disaster reconstruction trajectory.

key_developments.title: Vanuatu citizenship-by-investment + economic-citizenship framework
key_developments.description: Vanuatu citizenship-by-investment programme expanded 2018 — substantively distinctive Pacific Island economic-citizenship framework affecting subsequent cross-border-jurisdiction-practice and family-law-mobility framework. Subsequent EU visa-free-list removal 2024 substantively significant cross-border-jurisdiction-impact.

key_developments.title: ICJ climate-change-advisory-opinion request + Vanuatu-leadership framework
key_developments.description: Vanuatu-led ICJ advisory-opinion request on climate change 29 March 2023 (UN General Assembly Resolution 77/276) — substantively distinctive globally Vanuatu-leadership-in-climate-international-law framework. Substantive Pacific Islands Forum framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Vanuatu earthquake December 2024 + reconstruction-trajectory framework
key_developments.description: Vanuatu earthquake 17 December 2024 substantively affecting Port Vila — substantively distinctive Pacific seismic-disaster framework affecting demographic and family-law-implementation framework through post-disaster reconstruction trajectory affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Climate ICJ Advisory Opinion + Vanuatu Christian Council + Family Protection institutional framework
key_developments.description: Vanuatu-led ICJ advisory-opinion request on climate change 29 March 2023 (UN General Assembly Resolution 77/276) — substantively distinctive globally Vanuatu-leadership-in-climate-international-law framework. Vanuatu Christian Council and Department of Women's Affairs institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Family Protection Act 2008 + Control of Marriage Act + kastom-customary-law framework. Substantively significant Pacific institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Court of Appeal of Vanuatu + Island Courts — welfare-of-the-child substantive register + Charlot Salwai presidency
key_developments.description: Court of Appeal of Vanuatu and Island Courts continue to develop welfare-of-the-child jurisprudence under Family Protection Act 2008 + Control of Marriage Act + Maintenance of Children Act + kastom-customary-law framework in custody disputes within Charlot Salwai (RMC) presidency since 5 October 2024. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Vanuatu operates the only Anglo-French dual-colonial-inheritance mixed-legal-system framework globally within the corpus — English common-law substantive + French civil-law substantive influence + kastom customary-law via Island Courts. Unique structurally distinctive position.
Trilingual official-language framework (Bislama + English + French) reflects the Anglo-French Condominium heritage — structurally distinctive within the Pacific cluster.
Non-Hague Convention status places Vanuatu in the non-Hague Pacific cluster.
editorial_notes: Vanuatu jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Anglo-French-Condominium-to-Salwai trajectory: 1906-Anglo-French-Condominium-established + 1980-Vanuatu-independence-+-Walter-Lini + 1983-Island-Courts-Act + 1993-UNCRC-ratification + 2008-Family-Protection-Act + 2015-Cyclone-Pam + 2018-Vanuatu-citizenship-by-investment + 2023-ICJ-climate-change-advisory-opinion-request + 2024-Vanuatu-earthquake-+-reconstruction + 2024-Court-of-Appeal-+-Island-Courts-+-Salwai-presidency.

Anglo-French-dual-colonial-inheritance mixed-legal-system Pacific Island (English common-law substantive + French civil-law substantive + kastom customary-law via Island Courts + trilingual Bislama-English-French + Family Protection Act 2008 + non-Hague Convention).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive welfare-of-the-child analysis under Family Protection Act 2008 + kastom-customary-law framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Pacific-Island + only-Anglo-French-dual-colonial-inheritance-mixed-legal-system-framework-globally-distinctive + only-Anglo-French-Condominium-in-history-1906-1980-distinctive + trilingual-official-language-Bislama-English-French + kastom-customary-law-Island-Courts-Act-1983 + Walter-Lini-founding-PM-1980 + Cyclone-Pam-2015-Category-5-Pacific-climate-disaster + Vanuatu-citizenship-by-investment-+-EU-visa-free-list-removal-2024 + ICJ-climate-change-advisory-opinion-request-2023-Vanuatu-leadership-international-climate-law-distinctive + Vanuatu-earthquake-2024 + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
